title: Phase 4d: iOS client
description:
iOS client sharing the Flutter codebase established in nie-wtz (Android). Same nie-core Rust library, different platform targets and OS integrations.

## Scope

Shares with Android (nie-wtz):
- Flutter UI: Chat, Contacts, Wallet, Settings screens
- flutter_rust_bridge bindings to nie-core
- Business logic: payment flow, wallet sync, identity

iOS-specific work:
- Cross-compile targets: aarch64-apple-ios, aarch64-apple-ios-sim, x86_64-apple-ios (simulator)
- Xcode project setup and provisioning profiles
- Identity key storage: iOS Keychain (SecItemAdd/SecItemCopyMatching) instead of Android Keystore
- Push notifications: APNs (Apple Push Notification service) for background wake-up when persistent WebSocket is killed by iOS app lifecycle
- Background modes: Background Fetch + VoIP socket trick for persistent connection while backgrounded
- App Store submission: App Store Connect, TestFlight for beta, export compliance (uses encryption → ERN required)
- Privacy manifest (PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy): declare no data collection, no tracking

## Key differences from Android
- iOS kills background processes aggressively — VoIP socket or background task must be used to keep relay connection alive
- Keychain access requires entitlements (com.apple.keychain-access-groups)
- App Store review is slower and stricter than Play Store; encryption use requires export compliance documentation
- APNs requires a paid Apple Developer account ($99/yr)

## Dependencies
- nie-wtz (Android client) must be complete first — Flutter project and flutter_rust_bridge bindings are established there
- nie-hlns (JSON-RPC protocol) must be stable — iOS client implements the updated protocol
